reviews.review_title: Great game so far one small issue…
reviews.review_description: I’ve been experiencing a bug with the mini boss level. I go to use kylin mode and press the ultimate attack button and it works perfectly fine, but then the game legitimately gets stuck on the mini boss level and it won’t end every time I do it

I saw a vid on Instagram of this games jiggle physics. Upon seeing it was only £5 I purchased and I DID NOT regret it. 10/10 would recommend if you like the jiggle between agonising boss fights. Bit of an update here after you've responded I have found two glitches not game breaking but certainly annoying that I didn't bring up previously. During the launch sequence where you hop in the circle thing and launch out the hanger. Just before your about to launch you can look behind you and the mech model is still there. The second glitch is a bit more awkward to replicate. I've got pretty bad stick drift so I always disable turning and use my own body to turn. During the sequence before combat where the mech is jetting through the scenery it always spawns in and gets stuck at an angle. (if you want footage / screenshots lucasbonsey20@gmail

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review and the update with the extra details.
Yeah, the Instagram clip of Maki's physics is what got a lot of people to click "buy" at £5,glad the actual game didn't disappoint and you're enjoying the boss fights (and the jiggle in between).
On the two glitches:

Launch sequence: Being able to look behind you and still see the full mech model right before blasting off is a known visual overlap. It's harmless but breaks the illusion a bit. We'll clean that up so the model hides properly during the final moments of the sequence.
Jetting-through-scenery sequence with stick drift disabled: When you turn physically instead of using the stick, the mech sometimes spawns at a weird angle and gets stuck. That one's trickier to reproduce consistently, but since you offered footage/screenshots at lucasbonsey20@gmail, feel free to send them over (or upload to Discord)  it'll help us pin it down faster.

We'll get both fixed in one of the next patches. Appreciate you taking the time to report them even after the initial review.
If anything else pops up, or you just want to talk mecha builds, come by our Discord: https://discord.gg/VuNyZQQZKH
Mecha Force Team

reviews.review_title: Decent game for The price but(4 updates
reviews.review_description:
Love the game. I didn't realize you had to do the challenges in the command room in order to get the currency to upgrade the mech attack, HP, Dash and defense though that set me back a bit. It would also be nice to actually warp to the upgrade screen at any time while not in combat. I have to die in order to go upgrade stuff so that's a little strange, just being honest. Picking up the Big swords is kind of difficult not gonna lie. Picking those up or the big shield or the spear is too hard to do and I'm dead by the time I actually pick them up so a mechanism to make it so you point, squeeze the trigger and they just come to you and you're automatically gripping them would be ideal. The spear sucks also. Can't even hold that thing right enough to attack with it because you pick it up upside down. Super, Super irritating. The jiggle physics is to die for! I hope that improves a whole lot. Like a whole, whole lot haha. I really like this game and I'm REALLY happy about Piloting a huge Mech like this! Super fun. Just fix the Weapons you test out or whatever. Can't easily pick those up at all. I'm always half dead or about to be dead by the time I'm actually able to pick them up.

Update: Kudos to you guys for fixing the Big sword problem! I recently played and it worked flawlessly. Very happy with that. Also the jiggle physics improved a bit as well! I definitely noticed haha. Upgrading to 5 stars because you guys are cool af

Update 2: I noticed on the tundra missions that if i dash at an enemy who's on a mountain top i either get stuck on the mountain and can't get off or i go through the mountain and can't get out, unable to complete the mission. Also a few times when i go into Kylin mode too fast after activating an ability the options don't pop up, everything slows down but I can't pull a lever or push a button. Also one last thing picking up the dimensional weapons is better but if i fail to pick up the weapon by pressing the trigger too many times to try to puck it up it sortve short circuits and explodes out of frame causing the weapon to disappear.

Update 3: Seriously get rid of the chainsaw and spear weapons. Those are the absolute game killers of this game. Every other weapon is fun and exciting except for those two. They have no place in this game whatsoever and could be easily replaced by such cooler weapons. Also sometimes when I'm doing one of those extra missions for white currency in-between a Main missions the game just wont give them to me for some reason. Like I'll start out with only 50 when i start, I'll choose the mission that pays out 300 white currency and at the end of it I'll still just have 50 and can't upgrade anything. It's happened about 5 or 6 times now and it's super weird. One last thing is it just impossible to get some of the Resonance Upgrades? Mysterious Turtle is just that. Mysterious because it very rarely shows up and when it does it's only like 2 or 3 times. Bai ze(the one that's not Bi fang)i think is another one that seems sortve impossible to get too. I've gotten all of them except for those two specifically and my curiosity is killing me.

Update 4: So i reset the game towards mission 4 on the polar levels and it just straight up killed my progress and made me start over. I'm just trying to see what these resonance upgrades are, dude. Wtf does Bai Zeh do? What do they ALL do? Come out with a post or something because this game's starting to piss me off. I've been resetting the game to get favorable upgrades(white currency missions) JUST to figure out what some of these stupid resonance upgrades do with no problems until just now. Haven't even figured out what Bai Zeh does in what the past 6 or 7 playthroughs? Mysterious turtle is just nothing we'll ever get? This stuffs getting annoying. If resetting the game kills progress now then I'll be done with this noise. Forget getting 100 damage and health fully upgraded and forget making rank. My progress being killed like that was absolutely unacceptable... Back down to 4 stars smh

Update 5: Ok something game breaking keeps happening. I beat the 1st boss in the polar mine area(the one that summons two minions to fight for him on bloodbath difficulty) and didn't kill off his two minions and they end up Hitting me after the currencies are totalling up and all of a sudden I'm knocked out of frame by the two mechs and can't upgrade and move on. I have to reset the game every single time and rematch the cruddy boss. The two Mechs he summons should immediately disappear/be destroyed upon victory to prevent this from happening. Is that doable?

developer_response.body: The effect of Bai Ze is: 25% chance to use combat skills without consuming any energy. Thank you for all your comments above. As a developer, it's a great honor to see our game being played with such dedication and care.

reviews.review_description: I think its odd that the hands of the kylin are dispositioned from the controller and i don't like how the camera is in a fixed position when inside of the kylin. I want my 6 degrees of freedom! And also I noticed that in the game over screen those death messages arent just plain png's but are cubes with 6 faces which is a bit odd for optimization. And i dislike not being able to move while maki is talking. I'd also like a playerbody sitting in the mech for immersion but other than that, great game so far, i love all the mech stuff.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the detailed review and for the honest nitpicks this kind of specific feedback is genuinely useful while we're still polishing the experience.
KYLIN hands offset from controllers: Yeah, a few players have mentioned the mecha hands feeling a bit misaligned with their physical controllers. It's not intentional drift, but something in the mapping that can feel off depending on your setup or playstyle. We'll look at tightening the calibration and possibly adding a small hand offset slider in settings if it helps more people. If it's particularly bad for you (e.g. forward/back, height, or rotation), let me know your headset and roughly how it feels so we can reproduce it better.
Fixed camera position in the cockpit / wanting full 6DoF: This one comes up regularly. Right now the cockpit view is deliberately locked for that "seated in a giant robot" feeling we wanted it to feel like you're strapped into the pilot seat rather than floating freely inside the mech. That said, I completely get wanting more freedom to lean, look around, and shift your viewpoint naturally. Full 6DoF head movement inside the cockpit is something we're discussing for future updates because immersion like that matters to a lot of pilots. No promises on timeline yet, but it's noted.
Game over screen with 3D cubes instead of flat images: Good eye on the optimization side. Those death messages being full 6-faced cubes instead of simple 2D sprites is a bit wasteful, especially on Quest hardware. It's a leftover from early prototyping that never got fully cleaned up. We'll swap them to proper optimized UI elements in an upcoming patch small win for performance.
Can't move while Maki is talking: This is a fair complaint. The dialogue moments are meant to give her lines some weight, but locking player movement breaks the flow for many. We'll look at allowing free movement (or at least limited head/body movement) during her talks so it feels less like a cutscene pause. Same goes for other hangar interactions if they're too restrictive.
Player body sitting in the mech for immersion: Solid suggestion. Adding a visible pilot body (arms, torso, maybe some cockpit straps) is on the immersion wishlist. It would help sell the scale and "you're really in there" feeling even more. We'll see how feasible it is without adding too much visual clutter or performance cost.
Overall though, really glad the core mech action and piloting are clicking for you that's the heart of the game. These are all polish items we can improve over time.
If any of these issues feel particularly bad in your current runs, or if you have more details/suggestions, drop them here. We're reading every review like this.
Appreciate you taking the time.
If you'd like to share more feedback or ideas, feel free to join our Discord: https://discord.gg/VuNyZQQZKH
Mecha Force team

reviews.review_title: The sole hope of the anime robot game
reviews.review_description:
Many VR robot games have been released, but they all featured wild, MechWarrior-style robot designs, which couldn't resonate with the hearts of the generation that grew up watching Japan's Gundam and Getter Robo. This game, right from the robot's  appearance sequence, makes the hearts of anime enthusiasts burn hot! You insert the key, turn it, grab the propulsion levers with both hands, pull them, and launch! This is a developer who truly understands the romance of a robot's launch scene. Many version updates have been made from the demo to the official release, and I'm really looking forward to future updates.

The only slightly disappointing point is the narrowness of the map space. I hope that maps allowing players to face many enemies in a wide area, like in ZOE2 or Armored Core, will be added, perhaps even as DLC, moving away from the overly cramped maps. If there were maps like this, I think multiplayer would also be possible. And I really hope that the various languages mentioned in the roadmap will be added soon.

developer_response.body:
Hey, this review just made my day.
Reading "the sole hope of the anime robot game" hits different when you've been grinding to get the KYLIN's launch sequence right the key turn, the levers, that exact feeling of ignition. We knew a lot of VR mecha games went full Western military-industrial, but we wanted something that actually felt like it came from the same bloodline as Gundam and Getter Robo. Hearing that the startup scene lands for someone who grew up with those shows means a lot. Thank you.
We're genuinely glad the updates from the demo to full release have been noticeable too. We've been iterating hard on weapons, synergies, enemy behavior, and that true ending since launch.
On the map size: yeah, I hear you loud and clear. The current arenas are intentionally tight and intense for the roguelike pacing, but the "cramped" feeling is something several players have called out, especially when comparing to the wide-open chaos of ZOE2 or Armored Core. Bigger, more open maps with room to maneuver against swarms of enemies is exactly the kind of direction we're discussing for future content (DLC or major updates). Multiplayer would be incredible if we can make the scale work, but that's still further down the line.

reviews.review_title: True Cockpit Experience with a Giant Mec
reviews.review_description:
This game truly gives the feeling of piloting a giant robot from within the cockpit, not just controlling a powered suit.
Many games with motion-controlled arms can sometimes feel more like powered armor, but this one captures the essence of a true mecha experience — and that feeling is incredibly important to me.

I have just one humble request, if possible:
Due to a condition with my fingers, it would mean a lot if the Beam Sword could be used with a toggle option in addition to the current hold-to-activate method.
Thank you very much for your consideration.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for leaving this review and especially for explaining why the cockpit feel matters so much to you.

That distinction you made is exactly what we aimed for: not just swinging motion-controlled arms like powered armor, but actually sitting in a proper giant robot cockpit with all the weight and presence that comes with it. Hearing that it landed for you means a lot.

On the Beam Sword: I appreciate you bringing this up with the specific reason (your finger condition). Hold-to-activate works for most people, but it’s not ideal for everyone. Adding a toggle option for the Beam Sword (and potentially other hold-based actions) is a really reasonable accessibility request. I’ve passed this straight to the team it’s now on our list for the next few updates.

We’ll look at making it a simple on/off toggle in the settings so you can choose what feels best. If there are any other controls or actions that cause similar issues for you, feel free to let me know and we’ll consider those too.

Thanks again for playing and for the thoughtful feedback. We want Mecha Force to be playable and enjoyable for as many pilots as possible.

Keep enjoying the KYLIN and sorry in advance if the next update takes a little time to land.

reviews.review_description:
I haven’t played enough to give a full in depth review, but it’s pretty fun so far. The only complaint I have is I can’t seem to remove the blinders when turning. I’m a VR vet so it only serves to distract me. 
And 1 question: im on level 4 of my run right now, but im transported to this area after every level where I can’t do anything. The helper tells me I can upgrade stuff there but nothing pops up. 

Also, 1 other minor complaint… it takes too long between rounds. I fight for 1-2 mins, then have 3-5 mins of downtime. Wish there was an option to immediately advance to the next level without transporting to home base…. Unless there’s something I’m supposed to be doing at home base?

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review and for sharing your early impressions glad you're having fun with it so far.
A couple of quick clarifications on the points you raised:

Blinders when turning: Totally understand as a VR vet you probably have strong VR legs and the artificial turning vignette can feel unnecessary or distracting. We do have an option to disable or reduce the blinders/vignette. Go into Settings → Comfort → Turning Vignette (or similar the exact label might be "Vignette on Snap Turn" or "Movement Vignette"). Set it to Off or Low. If you can't find it, let me know what headset you're on and I can give more precise steps.
The hub area after every level: That's intentional for now. After clearing a stage you're brought back to the KYLIN's hangar (the "home base") where you're supposed to be able to spend upgrade points on weapons, abilities, and permanent progression. If nothing is popping up and the helper is just standing there saying you can upgrade but the menu isn't appearing, that sounds like a bug. A few players have reported similar issues, especially on certain runs or after specific bosses. Quick things to try:
Make sure you're walking up close to the helper/NPC and looking directly at them (sometimes the interaction prompt needs a clear line of sight).
Try using the grab/interact button on the upgrade console or panels around the hangar if visible.
If it's still dead, a quick restart of the run or the game usually fixes it. We'll look into making the upgrade trigger more reliable.

Downtime between rounds: Yeah, the 3-5 minutes of "hangar time" after short 1-2 minute fights is a common complaint, especially for players who want a faster pace. Right now the flow forces the return to base so you can choose upgrades and see your build evolve. We get that it breaks momentum for some. An option to skip the hangar / quick launch to next stage (with maybe auto-spending upgrade points or a simplified upgrade screen) is something we're actively discussing for a future update. It won't be in the very next patch, but it's on the radar because of feedback like yours.

If you're still stuck in the hub with no upgrades showing, drop me a bit more info (headset type, roughly how far into the run, any error messages?) and I'll try to help troubleshoot or get it reported properly.
Thanks again for playing early feedback like this is gold while we're still shaping the pace and flow.
Keep piloting.
If you'd like to share more feedback or ideas, feel free to join our Discord: https://discord.gg/VuNyZQQZKH
Mecha Force team

This game is a blast to play, for the price you are getting quite alot and since this is just the release im sure theres plenty more to come, from the fluidity of movement and fast paced action its genuinely probably one of my top 5 games right now.

With that said i have 1 issue so far, its the vignette with the smoove turning, i looked everywhere on the settings menu but nothing would change it , everytime i look around i feel like im looking through a spy glass cutting me out of the immersion. Yes i can use snap turning but the annoyance of having to constantly tap my controller left or right just to turn to a direction that could potentially not even be the exact view i want can get quite irritating in moments when i need to be more precise with my movements. 

Yes i can always just turn around and thats what i do, but for the people who dont have the luxury like me to have so much space and want smooth turning , it could be quite frustrating.

Other than that im having a great time keep up the great work.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review glad you're having a blast with the game and that the fluid movement and fast action are landing so well for you. Getting called one of your top 5 right now is high praise, especially this early after release. We're planning plenty more content and polish going forward.
On the smooth turning vignette issue: I get why it's breaking immersion for you. As a VR vet with limited play space, having that constant "spyglass" tunnel effect while using smooth turning is annoying as hell, and snap turning isn't a great workaround when you need precision.
The setting does exist, but it can be easy to miss depending on how the menu is laid out in the current version. Try this path:

Pause the game and go into Settings
Look for World Movement, Locomotion, or Movement section (sometimes under a General or Comfort tab)
Inside there should be a Comfort subsection with options for Turning Vignette / Comfort Assistance / Vignette Strength (usually High / Medium / Low / Off)

Set the vignette for turning/movement to Off. That should remove the black edges when you smooth turn.
If you still can't find it after checking those spots (or if changing it does nothing), let me know exactly which headset you're using and roughly what the settings menu looks like for you we'll get it reported as a visibility or bug issue and push a fix.
In the meantime, physical turning in place is what a lot of players fall back on, but we definitely want smooth turning to feel good without the forced comfort aids for experienced users.
Appreciate you calling it out clearly. Stuff like this helps us make the experience better for everyone who wants that full cockpit freedom.
Keep enjoying the KYLIN and thanks again for the support.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review.It's great to hear you're enjoying how the game keeps getting better the more you play.
The headquarters unlocking and the way death brings new features is one of the core roguelike twists we built in, so I'm glad that "interesting twist" is clicking for you. It rewards pushing further even when runs end badly.
On bigger maps: This keeps coming up and you're right ,the current mission spaces are compact for that intense, close-quarters roguelike pacing, but we definitely want to expand into larger, more open arenas where you can really maneuver, kite, and take on bigger groups of enemies. It's high on the list for future content.
Your suggestions for intermittent mission objectives (defend/capture-style goals like "enemy must not destroy X") are spot on. They would give us natural beats to push the story forward during the action instead of just in the hangar. Adding proper animated cutscenes or discourse tied to those objectives is something we've been talking about internally to give the tale more weight. Same with an AI co-op companion fighting alongside you that could add a lot of fun and tactical depth without breaking the solo focus.
These are the exact kind of ideas that help shape the next major updates. We're still adding weapons, synergies, enemies, and systems, so bigger maps + richer objectives + more narrative delivery are all in the conversation.
If you keep running into anything else (good, bad, or just ideas) as you play more, drop it here. Feedback like this is what moves the needle.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review. I really appreciate you giving the game a shot even though mech games aren't usually your thing.
Glad the combat ended up being enjoyable for you, and that the roguelike customization/upgrade systems kept things interesting enough to pull you in. The environmental design getting a nod is nice too we tried to make the arenas feel distinct and fitting for the anime-mecha vibe.
On the scaling/depth perception issue with the KYLIN: yeah, that's a fair point. A few players have mentioned that the giant mech scale can sometimes mess with depth perception (especially when judging distances for melee swings or ranged shots). It's a tricky balance in VR because we want that big, powerful feeling without making aiming feel off. We'll keep an eye on it and may look at small tweaks to the cockpit view or visual cues in future updates to help with that.
Thanks again for playing and for the honest take. Recommendations from people who don't normally go for this genre mean a lot.
If you keep playing and run into anything else (good or bad), feel free to drop another note.
Keep enjoying the runs.
Appreciate the feedback if you're up for it, we'd love to hear more from you on our Discord: https://discord.gg/VuNyZQQZKH
Mecha Force team

reviews.review_description:
Mecha force isn't quite what I had hoped it would be but it is a solid start. I had hoped that mecha force would be somewhere between gundam and neon genesis, epic struggles between powerful foe,  but it didn't feel that way for me, more like a turret shooter rather than monsterous war machine. The art style is strong and consistent, leaning toward the lean mecha of neon genesis and it carries through the player environments and enemy designs, which is great but I would like to see more depth given to the environment as that would have helped with the immersion. 

The immersion while piloting the mech is also hammered and borders on the frustrating, if you dont want to feel like a turret. Rotational controls can be rapid, however movements which are lateral feel like they are stuck in the mud, sluggish but without out the weight of momentum which encourages turret like interactions even when playing with duel energy swords. spinning on the spot batting shots back and waiting for target to close and finish their attack pattern rather than close tustles of parrying and attacking.

On the technical front, while the style is consistent I found the frame rate to struggle with the rotational speed, even when I reduced that speed I didn't see an improvement.  This maybe better if playing on a quest 3 rather than quest 2 so it's something worth keeping in mind. I'd also commend playing in a seated position rather than standing if you don't have good 'vr legs' as rotational frame rate can have a signicant effect on balance. 

In summary if you want a mech styled blast fest, then you're well set. However if you want the grace of gundam or the weight of titanic war machines it might not scratch it itch, but it is something to try and see how you feel.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share such a thoughtful and detailed review. We really appreciate your perspective on both the strengths and areas where Mecha Force can grow.

Your points about immersion, combat flow, and movement balance are very important to us. We want players to feel like they’re truly piloting a powerful war machine, not just a stationary turret, and feedback like yours helps guide how we refine controls and mechanics to get closer to that vision. Performance concerns, especially on Quest 2, are also on our radar, and we’re continuing to optimize across all supported devices to deliver smoother, more responsive gameplay.

We’re also looking into ways to expand environmental depth and interactivity to strengthen immersion further. It’s clear how much you value that Gundam- or Evangelion-like sense of scale and intensity, and it’s something we’re actively striving to capture as development continues.

If you’d like to be part of the discussion and help us shape these improvements, we’d love to invite you into our community Discord: https://discord.gg/VuNyZQQZKH
. Having players like you sharing feedback directly with the dev team makes a big difference.

We hope that as Mecha Force evolves, we’ll earn the chance for you to revisit your review in the future. Thanks again for giving the game a try and for helping us improve it.

— Ming Studio

reviews.review_description: Mecha force is a really fun foray into the vr mecha genre. It features beautiful stylized cel-shaded graphics. The gameplay is a wonderful mix of melee and ranged combat. The way your hands line up with the mecha as you view the action from the cockpit is really immersive. This game is very reminiscent of Zone of the Enders on psv1 and pcvr, except it just runs beautiful and smooth on standalone. I would highly recommend this to any fans of classic anime like Robotech or Evangelion.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review ,this one genuinely made me smile.
Hearing "90s anime mecha fans rejoice" is exactly the kind of energy we were going for. We poured a lot into the cel-shaded style, cockpit immersion, and that blend of melee punches + ranged blasting to capture the spirit of classic anime mecha like Zone of the Enders, Robotech, and Evangelion. The fact that the hand-to-mecha alignment and cockpit view feel properly immersive to you is huge that's one of the hardest things to get right in VR.
Super glad it runs smooth and beautiful on standalone too. We optimized hard for that because we wanted this to feel like a proper home for fans who grew up on those PS2-era mecha classics but now want it in VR.
Appreciate the high recommendation. If you're deep into the runs and notice anything else (weapons that feel especially good, parts of the combat that could use more love, or even more anime nods you'd like to see), I'd love to hear it.
Keep enjoying the KYLIN and welcome to the club.
If you want to chat about the game, suggest features, or help test future updates, come join our Discord server: https://discord.gg/VuNyZQQZKH
Mecha Force team

reviews.review_title: Good but not 100% what I expected
reviews.review_description: I love VR games that implement ways to experience VR and, in this case, playing as a person controlling a mech. I found it surprising and cool at first but after playing a bit it got kind of boring, there is definitely a learning curve. I lost a few times on levels I feel I shouldn't have. There were tons of things in the game, I don't know if it was too much to the point of overwhelmingness or if it was just my feel for the game. It has great vibrant and detailed maps, and I even enjoyed the voiced dialogue. Altogether, if you want an interesting way to play VR and you like mechs, this game is 100% for you but this is not a top pick for me. (I'm very picky when it comes to gaming on oculus)

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the honest review.
I get it the initial “wow, I’m actually controlling a giant mech in VR” feeling was cool, but after a while the loop started to feel a bit boring for you. The learning curve is real, and those deaths on levels that felt like they shouldn’t have happened can be frustrating. We know the amount of systems and upgrades can sometimes feel overwhelming at first too.
The vibrant maps and voiced dialogue getting positive mentions is good to hear though. We’re still early and actively working on tightening the pacing, making the early game more approachable without losing depth, and adding more variety to keep runs feeling fresh.
Appreciate you saying it’s still a solid pick for anyone who wants that mech-in-VR experience, even if it’s not a top one for you personally (especially as a picky Oculus player). Honest feedback like this helps us improve.
If you want to share more specific thoughts on what felt boring or overwhelming (combat feel, upgrade choices, mission length, etc.), feel free to join our Discord: https://discord.gg/VuNyZQQZKH
Mecha Force Team

reviews.review_description:
Heavy Evangelion and Gundam influence for anime mecha fans. The combat is a mix of melee and gunplay, allowing you to equip different melee weapons (fists, sabers), projectiles (rockets, bullets, plasma etc.) and ultimates such as artillery and bombardments. 

Gameplay consists of very short arena levels facing off against a few mechs and flying enemies, every level only lasts a minute ending with a choice of 3 upgrades. The main levels are for learning/testing how your weapons/loadout work, whereas the bosses are where the real challenge is and require you to play carefully and strategically, otherwise if you make a few mistakes you're dead in seconds, so think of it as a roguelite souls game with mechs. The load-out system and upgrades goes very deep so there's a lot of potential to use different playstyles that suit you.

As there are no long or endless levels you'll be constantly playing and stopping every minute, choosing and equipping upgrades then going back. Only the target practice training mode is endless but the game itself has no other modes other than the campaign, so you're forced to play the entire game in 1-2 minute chunks which can be very tiring when every other roguelite has much longer levels and modes. The combat is good enough and the loadout system is deep, but the shortcomings make it only recommended for anime mech fans as the gameplay levels are just a little too short, it could have been amazing with full-length levels and exploration, instead it's a constant start-stop gameplay loop. I also experienced many bugs, some game-breaking and requiring restarts.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed review.
Appreciate you breaking down the Evangelion/Gundam influence, the mix of melee and gunplay, and how the loadout system goes deep with different playstyles. It's good to hear the core combat and boss fights feel strategic and Souls-like for anime mech fans.
On the short arena levels and constant start-stop loop (1-2 minute fights → upgrade → repeat): yeah, this is one of the most common pieces of feedback we're getting. The current tight roguelite pacing was intentional at launch, but we agree that longer levels, more exploration, and less frequent returns to the hangar would make the flow much better for many players. Full-length missions and additional modes are high on the list for future updates.
We'll also look into the bugs you ran into (especially the game-breaking ones). If you can remember any specific ones or when they happened, that would help a lot.
Thanks for the honest take this kind of constructive criticism helps us prioritize what needs work.
If you want to share more details or ideas with the team, feel free to join our Discord: https://discord.gg/VuNyZQQZKH
Mecha Force Team

reviews.review_description: The display at the beginning of the game is really shocking. As a mecha fan, I'm quite fond of this kind of flexible mecha battles. Moreover, there are many types of weapons in the game. Especially the Rocket punch is extremely fun to play with! At first, I didn't know how to use the Titan, but after I mastered it, the sense of impact was wonderful. The game is a bit difficult, but it's still possible to beat it with some efforts. There is a female character in the game who looks really cute, but sometimes her animations are a bit stiff. I hope there can be more interactions with her. The details of the game still need to be polished. For example, sometimes the lightsaber doesn't cause any damage when hitting the enemies. I don't know whether it's designed like this or there's a bug. All in all, I'm looking forward to the content of the official version.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your support! We’re committed to packing even more value into MechForce at this price—the full release will be worth the wait! Join our Discord for sneak peeks: https://discord.gg/Pzf5xvzqPR. Let’s dream big!

reviews.review_description:
I really like Mech games and VR. They’re just perfect for the VR experience. 
This game has you controlling a giant humanoid robot similar to Gundam and Pacific Rim. It really nails the feeling and immersion of being in the cockpit. The scale of KYLIN is amazing. Right from the beginning menu when you’re standing in his hand. Even between rounds in the hanger with him in the background.
The gameplay is great with depth that will take a bit to learn and perfect. 
It starts off hard and a little frustrating just like many other roguelite games. 
It’s meant to happen to introduce you to new upgrading systems. And it’s done well using a cute navigator named Maki, who explains everything you need to know about operating KYLIN. 
Regular upgrades like damage, health, shield and Dodge.
Also upgrades to physical body parts like your arm where you can see the visual difference. 
A main mission to go through that gets easier as you upgrade to make it farther. Also mini challenges that earn you points for the upgrades. ￼
Really fun game overall. Whether you’re punching or using a light saber. Shooting guns, rockets, Perry, and dodging.
A must play for anyone that likes Mechs in VR or rogue lites
It plays so well and very polished. I can’t believe it’s still early access and this good ￼

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review. Really glad you enjoyed it more than you expected.
It's awesome to hear the cockpit immersion, the massive scale of the KYLIN (from the menu scene standing in his hand to seeing him in the hangar), and that true giant humanoid robot feeling (Gundam / Pacific Rim vibe) landed so strongly for you. That sense of actually being inside the mech is exactly what we wanted to deliver.
The gameplay depth, upgrades (including visible changes to body parts like the arms), Maki's guidance as the cute navigator, mini challenges, and the roguelite progression loop are all core to the experience, so it's great they clicked for you even with the initial difficulty spike.
Appreciate you calling out how polished it feels despite still being early access. We'll keep adding more weapons, synergies, enemies, and content to make it even better.
If you want to share more thoughts or ideas with the team, feel free to join our Discord: https://discord.gg/VuNyZQQZKH
Mecha Force Team

reviews.review_title: Potential for best Mecha roguelike game!

This game provides a fantastic Mecha gameplay with great combats mixing close combat (with fists and lightsabers), and range weapons (with rockets and even throwing your fist away) and special abilities.
The game also progresses through a great roguelike loop, complete with side challenges in order to train. The amount of content there is really impressive (upgrades for each parts of your Mecha, as well as updating its key characteristics and an interesting storyline). 
I think this game will easily become an absolute must-have once a few bugs have been corrected. It already feels quite polished, but there are still a few hiccups to be straighten for Mecha Force to become a 5 star must-have.
